game_name: Super Star Force: Jikūreki no Himitsu

design_philosophy.core_loop: Vertical scrolling shoot segments interrupted by ground-based dungeon exploration; time travel hub navigation with resource management (time units as fuel)
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing risk-reward of ship destruction (halves score/currency) against progression necessity; non-linear time travel creates decision paralysis about which era to visit
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Pilot a time-traveling starship to prevent planetary destruction by collecting mystical artifacts across multiple eras and defeating the final boss Gordess
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hybrid shmup-adventure-RPG fusion rejects pure arcade purity; non-linear progression allows sequence-breaking; dual endings based on player agency; score functions as literal in-game currency rather than leaderboard metric

difficulty.continues_system: Unlimited continues available by pressing start after game over; player retains all possessions and currency but resets to year DA 2010
difficulty.checkpointing: Continue system effectively serves as checkpoint; persistent world state preserved across continues
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Non-linear era selection allows difficulty curve customization; players can choose easier eras first; time unit consumption creates implicit soft gates; score halving on ship destruction creates meaningful consequence without hard failure

progression.upgrade_system: Score functions as currency; higher score enables purchases in dungeons and time travel sequences; items acquired in dungeons (e.g., Solomon Key from Norm) unlock access to future eras
progression.unlock_mechanism: Seven time stones collected from hidden dungeons in each era unlock final confrontation; key items like Statue of Dorera open blocked passages; time unit expenditure gates access to distant eras
replayability_hooks: Dual endings (happy vs. sad) tied to player completion choices
Non-linear era traversal enables multiple solution paths
Hidden dungeons require thorough exploration to maximize stone collection
Score optimization loop for currency accumulation
Temporal causality: destroying Ganimu egg in DA 1301 prevents future city destruction

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling through era design (volcanic empire Erujinba, sunken Lemurian continent, Ice Age devastation)
Temporal causality mechanics: player actions in past eras affect future conditions
NPC interaction (Norm provides hints and context)
Architectural/mythological references (giant ground drawings of deity Birakocha, temple Jukai Madagu-Tem Oates)
Era naming convention (DA calendar system) establishes deep alternate history
Boss character mythology: evil planet Gordess as overarching antagonist driving narrative

boss_design.philosophy: Era-specific guardian bosses serve as dungeon culminations; Gordess as final boss represents ultimate temporal threat; boss encounters tied to story beats and stone collection
notable_bosses: Gordess (final boss, evil planet threatening timeline)
Ganimu (behemoth creature affecting multiple eras; can be prevented via temporal intervention in DA 1301)

faction_indicators: Boolean Shang city-state (DA 1608) - victimized by Ganimu behemoth
Primitive deity-worshipping culture (DA 0316) - hopes for divine return
Lemurian advanced civilization (DA 1301) - sunken, destroyed by geological forces
danger_signals: Gordess resurrection prophecy creates apocalyptic stakes
Ganimu behemoth emergence causes civilizational collapse
Volcanic eruptions and geological deformation threaten inhabited eras
Savage demons guarding key locations suggest territorial conflicts
environmental_storytelling: Volcanic crater access to underground slave empire
Giant ground drawings indicating cultural worship patterns
Sunken continent geography showing catastrophic historical events
Jungle temple architecture suggesting lost advanced societies
Ice Age civilization destruction revealing environmental apocalypse

sources_used: wikipedia: Super Star Force: Jikūreki no Himitsu
